title: Use node hostname in kube play when hostNetwork=true
body:
When the hostNetwork option is set to true in the k8s yaml, set the pod's hostname to the name of the machine/node as is done in k8s.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19321

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md

```release-note
Set pod hostname to node/machine name with kube play when hostNetwork=true in k8s yaml
```

comments.body: I would figure that it should set it within the uts namespace as well, if they don't then it is probably a bug.

I guess whenever you want a private uts depends on if your application tries to change the hostname in the container. If it does I would assume most people would not like to have it change the real hostname on the host which would likely not even be possible due missing CAP_SYS_ADMIN.

> CAP_NET_ADMIN not CAP_SYS_ADMIN

No that would make to much sense, according to the man page it uses SYS_ADMIN:
> EPERM  For sethostname(), the caller did not have the CAP_SYS_ADMIN capability in the user namespace associated with its UTS namespace (see namespaces(7)).


I would love if someone actually checks if k8s uses the host uts namespace or not. Also one thing that just came to my mind, the user can set a specific hostname via the yaml right? I would expect host network to still allow this but it looks like you set this afterwards which means it would overwrite the user value.

> Also one thing that just came to my mind, the user can set a specific hostname via the yaml right? I would expect host network to still allow this but it looks like you set this afterwards which means it would overwrite the user value.

I think this would be a bug or at least someone should tell me that k8s ignores the hostname arg when host netns is used.
If you are sharing the host network and setting the host name, then we should throw an error.  Podman should not be modifying the host network from the client.

As stated before hostname has nothing to do with the netns. The hostname is guarded by uts namesapce which is still private.

This is perfectly valid: 
```
$ podman run --network host --hostname abc alpine hostname
abc
```
I see no reason why the kube play should not allow that and always force the real hostname here. If a hostname has be explicitly set we should use it. Only if utsns is set to host then we error:
```
$ podman run --uts host --hostname abc alpine hostname
Error: invalid config provided: cannot set hostname when running in the host UTS namespace: invalid configuration

```

In k8s, when `hostNetwork:true` is set, you cannot set the `hostname` in the yaml. @Luap99 it does ignore the hostname arg when the host netns is used. I tested with podman kube play and the same thing happens here as well.

I am not seeing anything in the k8s docs about the uts namespace though so not sure about that.

> In k8s, when hostNetwork:true is set, you cannot set the hostname in the yaml. @Luap99 it does ignore the hostname arg when the host netns is used. I tested with podman kube play and the same thing happens here as well.

Ok then this change looks good. I would however like if you could add a test for the hostname field in the yaml and make sure that we overwrite with the node hostname as expected.

Also if you repush could add this as comment in there that it is expected that it overwrites an explicitly set hostname, because if somebody changes the order in the code that can start breaking.

I hate the fact that the Network namespace an UTC namespace are separate.

It seems like hostNetwork=true implies `--network=host --utc=host` We should make sure that happens for us as well.
Fixed to set UTS to host for this option. Added tests for this also
